recalls.Description: Lifetime Brands has issued a recall for their BUILT-branded LUUM light up tumblers, available in 16 oz. and 18 oz. sizes. These tumblers, featuring festive designs for winter holidays and the Fourth of July, contain an LED light powered by a button cell battery located in the bottom compartment. The tumblers are labeled with their theme names and corresponding item numbers, which can be found on the underside or hangtag.
recalls.Title: Lifetime Brands Recalls BUILT LUUM Light Up Tumblers Due to Choking and Battery Ingestion Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Lifetime Brands at 888-561-2269, Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at Recall@Builtny.com. Additional details can be found online at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall or by visiting www.builtny.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - FESTIVE FOREST
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - MERRY GLOW
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - LET IT SNOW
Recalled Built LUUM Tumbler - WINTER BLOOM
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - MERRY MAGIC
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - JOLLY JOURNEY
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - HEART N STARS
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - USA
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - STARS
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - FLAG
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - WINDMILL
Recalled Built LUUM LED Tumbler - BLUE FIREWORKS
"lumm," the tumbler's theme name, item number and UPC code are printed on a label located on the tumblers' underside or hangtag

Injuries.Name: Lifetime Brands has received one report of a child gaining access to the tumbler's button cell battery and putting it in the mouth. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Kroger, Hy-Vee, Meijer, Nordstrom Rack, and Winn-Dixie stores nationwide, as well as online at Builtny.com from October 2024 through December 2025, priced between $10 and $15.

Importers.Name: Lifetime Brands, Inc., owner of the BUILT brand, of Garden City, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of choking and ingestion from accessible button cell batteries due to potential breakage of the tumblers.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the tumblers, keep them out of children's reach, and properly dispose of the battery. Contact Lifetime Brands for a full refund, providing a photo of the discarded product when submitting the Product Recall Refund Form at https://www.builtny.com/pages/recall. Note: Dispose of button cell batteries according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: Lifetime Brands recalls BUILT LUUM light up tumblers due to choking and battery hazards. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description:
Walmart has announced a recall for the Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Insulated Water Bottles, identified by model number 83-662 on the packaging. These silver stainless-steel bottles feature a black one-piece screwcap lid and measure 4.41 x 4.41 x 11.5 inches, with the Ozark Trail logo displayed on the side. The model number is not visible on the bottle itself.

The recall is prompted by safety concerns that the lid can eject forcefully, leading to serious impact and laceration risks when attempting to open the bottles after they have contained food or carbonated beverages, as well as perishable drinks like juice or milk over time.
recalls.Title: Walmart Issues Recall for Ozark Trail 64 oz Water Bottles Due to Potential Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled bottles immediately and reach out to Walmart for a full refund. For assistance, contact Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily or visit www.walmart.com/help or https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless Steel Insulated Water Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Ozark Trail 64 oz Stainless-Steel Insulated Water Bottle, model 83-662
One-Piece Screwcap Lid

Injuries.Name: Walmart has received three reports of consumers who were injured when struck in the face by a lid forcefully ejected from these bottles upon opening. Two consumers suffered permanent vision loss after being struck in the eye.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores nationwide and online at Walmart.com since 2017 for approximately $15.

Importers.Name: Olympia Tools International Inc., of Covina, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious impact and laceration from ejected lid

Remedies.Name: Full refund available; return to nearest Walmart store

recalls.metaDescription: Walmart recalls Ozark Trail 64 oz Water Bottles due to serious injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact Walmart for a refund.

recalls.Description: Direct Scaffold Supply has issued a recall for five-inch casters used in baker scaffolding. These casters facilitate the movement of scaffolding but may pose a safety risk. Included in this recall are several model numbers: CAS5SS4P+BFL, CAS5SS4P+AHD, MPISUS+BFL, MPISUS, MPISUSD+BFL, MPISUST+BFL, MPISU+BFL, MPISU, MPISU+1, MPISUS+AHD, and MPISU+AHD. Consumers should verify the heat codes stamped on the metal part of their casters, as certain codes indicate the need for replacement: E22WM483, C23WM320, E23WM320, K23WM320, and D22WM483. Model numbers can be found on the purchase receipt or packaging.
recalls.Title: Direct Scaffold Supply Recalls Baker Scaffolding Casters Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Direct Scaffold Supply at 800-729-6118 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at bakercasters@realtimeresults.net, or online at www.recallrtr.com/bakercasters or www.dss.net by selecting 'Baker Caster Recall.'

Products.Name: Direct Scaffold Supply Baker Scaffolding Casters

Images.Caption: Recalled casters (CAS5SS4P+BFL)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUS+BFL) (MPISUS)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUSD+BFL)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUST+BFL)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISU+BFL) (MPISU) (MPISU+1)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISUS+AHD)
Recalled casters for baker scaffolding (MPISU+AHD)
Heat Code Example

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of casters breaking or failing, including one injury where a caster failure led to a fall and bruising injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at Direct Scaffold Supply branches, Lowe's stores nationwide, and online at Granite.com, Homedepot.com, and Lowes.com from September 2022 to March 2025, with prices ranging from $280 to $1,600 for scaffolding and approximately $110 for casters sold in four-packs.

Importers.Name: Direct Scaffold Supply LLC, of Houston, Texas

Hazards.Name: Faulty spot welds may cause casters to break, leading to unstable scaffolding and potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled casters and contact Direct Scaffold Supply for free replacement instructions. The company will provide a prepaid shipping label and packaging for returning the faulty casters, and new replacements will be sent after the return.

recalls.metaDescription: Direct Scaffold Supply recalls baker scaffolding casters due to fall hazards. Check model numbers and heat codes for safety.

recalls.Description: Nazzaro is recalling Rebecca's Toys & Prizes bowling pin sipper cups due to excessive lead levels in the red tape, violating federal safety standards. These cups are white with red tape around the neck and feature a red plastic cap, along with a label displaying the item number "ITEM NO. PG1045" and the brand name. Lead exposure can pose serious health risks to young children.
recalls.Title: Nazzaro Issues Recall for Bowling Pin Sipper Cups Due to Lead Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Nazzaro at 800-777-2235 from 10 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at service@rebeccas.com. Additional details are available online at https://www.rebeccas.com/recall.html or by visiting www.rebeccas.com and clicking on the "Recall" link at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Bowling Pin Sipper Cups by Rebecca's Toys & Prizes

Images.Caption: Recalled Bowling Pin Sipper Cup
Recalled Bowling Pin Sipper Cup's label

Retailers.Name: Available at bowling alleys across the country from January 2018 to July 2019 for approximately $5.

Importers.Name: Nazzaro Enterprises Texas Inc., of Hurst, Texas

Hazards.Name: Lead poisoning risk due to excessive lead levels in the red tape

Remedies.Name: Stop using the cups immediately and keep them away from children. Contact Nazzaro for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Nazzaro recalls Bowling Pin Sipper Cups due to lead poisoning risks. Learn how to get a refund and keep your children safe.

recalls.Description: HALO is recalling its reusable tumblers featuring metal straws due to potential laceration hazards. The tumblers are available in four distinct designs: 'The Mermaid' (pink/green), 'Island Surf' (blue/yellow), 'Sailor Breeze' (tan/orange), and 'The Galaxy' (blue/pink). Each tumbler includes a tan lid and has a capacity of 24 ounces, with 'Wawa' printed on them. Consumers should take immediate action if they possess these products.
recalls.Title: HALO Issues Recall for Tumblers with Metal Straws Due to Laceration Risks; Available Only at Wawa Locations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach HALO at 855-425-6266 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at halo.safety@halo.com, or visit https://halo.com/safety-recall-24oz-mug/ or http://www.halo.com and select 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Tumblers with a metal straw

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of incidents, resulting in four laceration injuries to the hand and mouth.

Retailers.Name: Wawa locations across Alabama, Washington D.C., Delaware, Florida, North Carolina, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Maryland during August 2024 for approximately $13.

Importers.Name: HALO Branded Solutions Inc., of Sterling, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration from the metal straw

Remedies.Name: Stop using the metal straw and contact HALO for a free silicone straw replacement or return the tumbler for a $15 gift card refund.

recalls.metaDescription: HALO recalls tumblers with metal straws due to laceration hazards. Contact HALO for replacements or refunds.

recalls.Description: MM Products has announced a recall of JoyJolt Declan Single-Wall Glass Coffee Mugs, model number JG10242. These mugs, sold in a 16 oz. size as a set of six, are clear and feature a handle. The model number can be found on the product packaging and order confirmation details.
recalls.Title: MM Products Issues Recall for JoyJolt Declan Glass Coffee Mugs Due to Burn and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach MM Products at 888-569-5680, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall@joyjolt.com or visit https://joyjolt.com/pages/product-notices-recalls or www.joyjolt.com and click on 'Safety Recalls' for further information.

Products.Name: JoyJolt Declan Single-Wall Glass Coffee Mugs

Images.Caption: Recalled Declan Single-Wall Glass Coffee Mug

Injuries.Name: There have been 103 incidents of the recalled coffee glasses breaking at the base, resulting in 56 injuries, including 35 burns across the body from spilled hot liquids, and 21 cuts. Seven incidents required medical attention, including surgery and stitches.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com and MM Products Inc.'s website www.joyjolt.com from September 2019 to May 2022, priced between $20 and $25 for a set of six.

Importers.Name: MM Products Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn and laceration from breaking or cracking when filled with hot liquids.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mugs immediately and contact MM Products for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: MM Products recalls JoyJolt Declan Glass Coffee Mugs due to burn and laceration hazards. Stop using them and seek a refund.

recalls.Description: Nestlé USA has announced a recall of metallic-coated ceramic mugs included in various Starbucks-branded gift sets sold during the 2023 holiday season. These mugs, available in 11 oz. and 16 oz. sizes, were part of gift sets such as the Starbucks Holiday Gift Set with 2 Mugs, Starbucks Classic Hot Cocoa and Mug, Starbucks Peppermint and Classic Hot Cocoas and Mug, and Starbucks Holiday Blend Coffee and Mug.
recalls.Title: Nestlé USA Recalls Starbucks-Branded Metallic Mugs Due to Burn and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Nestlé USA at 800-681-1676 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.nestleusa.com/media/pressreleases/metallicmugrecall or https://www.athome.starbucks.com and click on 'Metallic Mug Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Starbucks Metallic Mugs from Holiday Gift Sets

Images.Caption: Recalled Starbucks Holiday Gift Set with 2 Mugs (As Sold)
Recalled Starbucks Holiday Gift Set with 2 Mugs (Close-up of Mug)
Recalled Starbucks Classic Hot Cocoa and Mug
Recalled Starbucks Peppermint and Classic Hot Cocoas and Mug
Recalled Starbucks Holiday Blend Coffee and Mug
Location of the gift set identifier code on the bottom of the mug

Injuries.Name: There have been 12 incidents of the mugs overheating or breaking resulting in 10 injuries, including nine severe burns/blisters on fingers or hands, and one cut on a finger. One incident required medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Available online and in-store at Target and Walmart, as well as through Nexcom (military retail outlets) from November 2023 through January 2024, priced between $10 and $20 depending on the gift set.

Distributors.Name: Nestlé USA Inc., of Arlington, Virginia

Hazards.Name: Burn and laceration risks from overheating or breaking when microwaved or filled with hot liquids.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the mugs immediately and return them for a full refund, either at the point of purchase or by contacting Nestlé USA.

recalls.metaDescription: Nestlé USA recalls Starbucks metallic mugs due to burn and laceration hazards. Stop using them and seek a refund now.

recalls.Description: Cascade Designs has issued a recall for specific camping cooking pots due to safety concerns. These anodized aluminum pots, available in 1- to 2-liter sizes, were sold individually and as part of various stove systems and kits. The affected models include the Reactor, Trail Lite, and PocketRocket Stove Kits, all featuring 'MSR' branding on the side.
recalls.Title: Cascade Designs Recalls Select Camping Cooking Pots Due to Burn and Scald Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Cascade Designs Inc. at 800-531-9531 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.msrgear.com/pot-recall.html. Alternatively, consumers can go to https://www.msrgear.com and click on the 'Recall Header' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: MSR Camping Cooking Pots
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 60,655 (In addition, about 22,320 were sold in Canada and about 35 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled MSR Reactor Accessory cooking pot (1 liter)
Recalled MSR Trail Lite pot (2 liter)
Recalled MSR Reactor Stove System (1.7 liter)
Recalled MSR PocketRocket Stove Kit

Injuries.Name: The company has received 22 reports of the pot handle either detaching or becoming loose, including two reports of burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at outdoor equipment retailers across the U.S. from January 2008 to October 2023, priced between $40 and $290.

Importers.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Distributors.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn and scald due to pot handle detachment

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pot and contact Cascade Designs for a free replacement handle/lifter, which is easy to install without tools.

recalls.metaDescription: Cascade Designs recalls camping cooking pots due to burn hazards. Contact for a free replacement handle and ensure your safety.
